meta.title: Yiwu
meta.subtitle: Districts & floor directory
meta.tagline: Yiwu is one market the size of a town: ~20 product worlds across six districts, every floor a different category. This is the district-by-floor map, on real coordinates.
meta.methodology: District locations are real coordinates from Google Places, converted from China's GCJ-02 datum to WGS-84 to sit correctly on the OpenStreetMap / CARTO basemap. District 6 (the GDT centre) opened in October 2025 and is not yet pinned. Floor layouts are compiled from public market directories and can shift over time; confirm the current directory with a local agent before a visit.

districts.name: District 1
districts.note: The oldest and most gift-shop-like district: toys, artificial flowers, fashion jewelry and seasonal decor. The classic place to start, and a full day on its own if you sell gifts or party goods.

floors.label: Factory-direct showrooms (Christmas & home decor)
Seasonal & Christmas decorations
Fashion jewelry & hair accessories
Toys & artificial flowers

districts.name: District 2
districts.note: The hardware-and-gadget district: bags and umbrellas on the bottom, hardware/tools/locks above, then kitchenware, small appliances, telecom and electronics. The most relevant district for hardware founders.

floors.label: Foreign-trade & export agencies, featured products
Electronics, luggage, watches; factory outlets — bathroom hardware & industrial tools
Kitchenware & sanitary, small appliances, telecom, electronics, watches, batteries, lighting
Hardware tools & fittings, electrical products, locks, vehicles
Suitcases, bags, umbrellas, rainwear, packing bags

districts.name: District 3
districts.note: Stationery, sports goods, cosmetics and eyewear. Smaller and densely packed with niche goods; the go-to for school/office supplies, beauty products and glasses.

floors.label: Imported commodities hall
Cosmetics, stationery & sports, zippers & buttons, apparel accessories; factory outlets
Cosmetics & beauty, mirrors & combs, clothing accessories
Office supplies & stationery, sports & outdoor equipment
Pens & ink, paper articles, eyewear & glasses

districts.name: District 4
districts.note: The textile and soft-goods giant, and the biggest district. Socks at the base (Yiwu is the world's sock hub), then daily knitwear, footwear, underwear and accessories. Essential for apparel-accessory and soft-goods buyers.

floors.label: Tourism shopping & factory outlets (underwear, swimwear, scarves, shoes, hats)
Bras, underwear, belts, scarves & shawls
Footwear, ribbons, laces, threads, wool, towels
Daily necessities, gloves, hats, knitted & cotton goods
Socks & leggings

districts.name: District 5
districts.note: The international and home district: an imports pavilion (incl. the Africa exhibition centre) on the ground floor, bedding and wedding supplies above, then auto/motorcycle accessories and pet supplies. Districts 1–5 connect by footbridge.

floors.label: Auto & motorcycle accessories, pet supplies, general merchandise
Curtains, knitted raw materials, wedding supplies
Bedding, wedding supplies, wigs
Imported goods — food, health, cloth, arts & crafts, Africa centre

districts.name: District 6
districts.stat: Opened Oct 2025 · Global Digital Trade Center · newly opened
districts.note: The newest district, the Global Digital Trade Center (GDT). Smart devices (drones, robots, AR/VR), fashion jewelry, beauty & skincare, baby & kids' goods, trendy toys and travel gear. Location not yet pinned here as it is brand new.

floors.label: Smart devices — drones, robots, AR/VR
Fashion jewelry & trendy toys
Beauty, skincare & baby/kids' daily-use goods
Travel & lifestyle goods

orientation.v: Yiwu is about 1 hour by high-speed rail from Hangzhou. The trade city is in Futian; Districts 1–5 are connected by footbridges. Most shops open roughly 9:00–17:00.
District 1 — toys, artificial flowers, seasonal decor.
District 2 — tools, locks, kitchenware, small appliances, electronics.
District 5 — imported goods, bedding, auto & pet.
District 6 / GDT — drones, robots, AR/VR, new categories.
Plan one district per day. A single floor can take hours.

notes: The complex is roughly 6.4 million m² with 75,000+ stalls — one floor alone can take hours, so pick a district and a floor before you go.
Booths are showrooms; most goods are made in the surrounding factory towns. Many stalls are factory outlets and most offer low MOQs.
Pre-locate suppliers on Yiwugo.com or 1688 before arriving, and budget for a translator/agent (~¥300–400/day) if you don't speak Mandarin.
Buying one or two pieces is expensive; the real price needs a wholesale quantity. Tuesday–Thursday are calmest; markets close around Spring Festival.
